title: rubus koehleri subsp. spinulatus
intro: Rubus koehleri subsp. spinulatus is a subspecies of thorny shrub native to East Asia, belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae) and commonly known as the thorny raspberry. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. The thorny raspberry has been found to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its health benefits, such as improving cardiovascular health, reducing inflammation, and boosting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed fresh or dried, or used as a topical application for skin issues.
